Together, we can hold onto our House majority, elect Democrats up and down the ballot, and fight for every LGBTQ+ person to be able to be who they are without fear. "" Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth | "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren Democrats All content (C) 2020 Warren Democrats, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

subject: Reminder: Virtual Pride Celebration with Rep. Sharice Davids, Dana Goldberg, and Charlotte Clymer!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dani for Illinois
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/72a3a382364b/reminder-virtual-pride-celebration-with-rep-sharice-davids-dana-goldberg-and-charlotte-clymer?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#
I wanted to make sure that you got my email last week about our upcoming Virtual Pride Celebration on the 27th! **You won't want to miss this event with special guests Charlotte Clymer, Dana Goldberg, and Rep. Sharice Davids. **

[Click here to reserve your spot!](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=279fe50fdb&e=140a9b81e7)

Having the support of both of these amazing LGBTQ trailblazers and icons means so much to me, and I hope that you will join them this Saturday night to celebrate Pride with us. Celebrating Pride with parades and festivals in June is something that I look forward to every year. **While those festivals and parades might be canceled this year, we can still celebrate all the progress the LGBTQ community has made, and continue to pursue full equality. **

Celebrating Pride might look different this year, but we'll make sure to keep you entertained with musical guests, drag shows, Dana Goldberg's hilarious comedy, and other performances all night long!

I look forward to seeing you there!

Dani Brzozowski
Democratic Nominee for Congress, IL-16

friend,

We wrote to you earlier this week and let you know — our end of quarter deadline is coming up at the end of the month on June 30th.

**This quarter, we need you more than ever.**

As Governor Holcomb has put 100% of his energy behind fighting COVID-19 and getting Indiana back on track, we’ve changed everything about the way we campaign. Just like the rest of our state, we’ve had to do our jobs at a distance.

_And because we haven’t been able to hold in-person events or normal fundraisers, we’re relying on our online grassroots community more than ever before._

Our One Indiana agenda has never been more important.

Hoosiers need strong, compassionate, and effective leadership not just right now, but for the next four years and beyond. We need someone who knows how to build an economy, create jobs, and keep Hoosiers safe.

You can help us secure the leadership that Indiana needs by chipping in any amount before the end of the quarter. Our finance team will be reaching out again later this month, but we wanted to give you a chance to chip in right now.

body:

Friend, Did you see Chris' email this morning? Tomorrow, June 23rd, there are two critical elections happening in NY27 and we need to make sure that every single Republican has a plan to GET OUT AND VOTE! As Chris said - make your plan tonight, find your polling place, and remind your friends and family to get out and vote in BOTH elections. [**Find your polling place here**](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/aoP763zLEC2CbVP9LEtov9yQ/8PR4VlHDMmV6cdfzVqfM0g) We need to ensure that this seat stays in the hands of a strong conservative leader who will fight alongside our President - and Chris Jacobs will do just that. Get out and vote for Chris Jacobs TWICE tomorrow - once in the special and once in the primary. Together let's reject the negative, nasty politics of Beth Parlato and Nate McMurray and give this region the leadership it deserves. Sincerely,
Team Jacobs
> \---------- Forwarded message --------- Friend, The critical _**primary and special elections for New York 's 27th Congressional district are tomorrow**_, and we need to make sure that **every single Republican voter is ready to GET OUT AND VOTE!** _**Make your plan to vote today, find your polling place, and remind your friends and family members to vote as well**_ - every single vote matters! [To find your polling place click here](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/Db0YYXo9Uti42n0Bb1xOCA/8PR4VlHDMmV6cdfzVqfM0g) **If you have trouble at the polls tomorrow call 716-800-2987** I entered this race over a year ago because I wanted to restore integrity to this seat, and bring strong, conservative, and experienced leadership to Western New York. Nancy Pelosi and her hand-picked candidate, Nate McMurray, are actively working against our values, against our President, and against our Country. **We cannot let this seat fall into the hands of a radical socialist like Nate - a man who has called supporters of our great President "traitors."** That's why these elections tomorrow are critical, and that is why we need to make sure that every single Republican in NY27 makes their voice heard! **Together, let 's give this district the representation it deserves, and let's give our President a strong ally.** I'm proud to be the only candidate to have his endorsement - and unlike Nancy, Nate, and AOC - _**I 'll proudly fight alongside President Trump.**_ _**Once again I urge you to come out and vote tomorrow! I humbly ask for your support in both the special and primary elections.**_ Thank you for your continued support - **now let 's win this!** Sincerely,
> Chris Jacobs

name: Christy Smith
subject: Welcome to On The Trail With Christy!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | 200622 On the Trail With christy | | | | |
# On The Trail With Christy
---
| | | Peter,

Welcome to the first edition of On The Trail With Christy -- a monthly update I'll be sending to our most dedicated supporters to keep them updated on the campaign, happenings in the district, and important events around the country.

June began with nationwide protests over the death of George Floyd, police brutality, and the need to still declare Black Lives Matter. We are ending the month with major Supreme Court decisions that will shape our country for decades.

In surprising but good news, the Supreme Court voted to recognize workplace protections for LGBTQ people and to uphold DACA -- the Obama-era program that protects Dreamers from deportation.

Both of these communities have been under constant attack from the Trump administration. So I hope the decisions give them a much needed breath of relief.

But we also know the work is not done. We must protect LGBTQ people from discrimination in all areas -- housing, education, public spaces, and more. And Dreamers and their families deserve a path to citizenship, not just a constant cycle of temporary reprieve.

There are more cases to come -- including a pivotal one on abortion rights that could be the beginning of the end of _Roe v. Wade_ , a real danger if ever there was one.

All of these events make me more determined than ever to dig in and do the hard work of flipping this seat blue in November. It is dangerous to have Mike Garcia, who is nothing but a lemming for Trump, representing our district in Congress. And while winning one race won't fix all of our problems, it's a damn good place to start.

With you in the fight,

\-- Christy
---
|
---

[**Contact us here**](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba55/29776869/9d9a40b/569ecfb2/3543541764/VEsHAA/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://www.christyforcongress.org/contact"). |
clean_body: | []() | | 200622 On the Trail With christy | | | | | On The Trail With Christy | | | Peter, Welcome to the first edition of On The Trail With Christy -- a monthly update I'll be sending to our most dedicated supporters to keep them updated on the campaign, happenings in the district, and important events around the country. June began with nationwide protests over the death of George Floyd, police brutality, and the need to still declare Black Lives Matter. We are ending the month with major Supreme Court decisions that will shape our country for decades. In surprising but good news, the Supreme Court voted to recognize workplace protections for LGBTQ people and to uphold DACA -- the Obama-era program that protects Dreamers from deportation. Both of these communities have been under constant attack from the Trump administration. So I hope the decisions give them a much needed breath of relief. But we also know the work is not done. We must protect LGBTQ people from discrimination in all areas -- housing, education, public spaces, and more. And Dreamers and their families deserve a path to citizenship, not just a constant cycle of temporary reprieve. There are more cases to come -- including a pivotal one on abortion rights that could be the beginning of the end of Roe v. Wade , a real danger if ever there was one. All of these events make me more determined than ever to dig in and do the hard work of flipping this seat blue in November. It is dangerous to have Mike Garcia, who is nothing but a lemming for Trump, representing our district in Congress. And while winning one race won't fix all of our problems, it's a damn good place to start. With you in the fight, \-- Christy | NEWS ON THE DISTRICT: | | "" "" "" | "" "" "" | | | | UPDATES FROM CHRISTY | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | | | | "" Christy Smith is a community leader from CA-25 who has fought for her neighbors on the school board and in the California state legislature. Now she needs our help to defeat Mike Garcia and flip this battleground House seat blue in November. Help elect a champion for our health care, our schools, and our rights. name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Press Secretary: Mayors need to protect their cities
body:
The rampant violence across American cities this past weekend claimed the lives of children as young as 3.

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=434b94060d&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • June 22, 2020

** Press Secretary: Mayors need to protect their cities
------------------------------------------------------------
The rampant violence across American cities this past weekend claimed the lives of children as young as 3. It ended the life of a young father. It left more than 100 people shot in Chicago alone.

When local leaders excuse or ignore violence, the consequences are fatal. When corporate media downplays the carnage to fit its narrative, the victim count grows.

🎬 Press Secretary: Violence on our streets is “absolutely inexcusable” (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=397660cc9b&e=3ee4985eea)

“We expect mayors to step up and do their job and governors to step up and do their job—in the mold of President Trump, who secured D.C. after a night of lawlessness two weeks ago,” Press Secretary Kayleigh McEnany said during a briefing this afternoon.

President Trump took additional action last week to promote safe policing and safe communities. His executive order reserves certain grant money only for law enforcement agencies that meet the highest standards of conduct, including in the use of force.

The President also supports Sen. Tim Scott’s (R-SC) legislation to increase police transparency, incentivize the use of body cameras, and withhold federal money from police departments that continue to permit the use of chokeholds by officers.

Americans want sensible, bipartisan justice reform modeled after President Trump’s First Step Act, which fixed unjust sentencing rules that disproportionately hurt the African-American community. Americans do not want politicians who bow down to the far-left movement to defund the police and excuse the deadly violence on our streets.

READ: President Trump works to bring police and communities closer together (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0dffa17de5&e=3ee4985eea)

** President Trump extends visa suspensions to protect U.S. workers
------------------------------------------------------------
President Trump is keeping his promise to put American workers first as our economy reopens following the global Coronavirus pandemic.

With a new proclamation signed today, the Administration is extending and expanding the suspension of certain immigration visas through the end of this year, ensuring that unemployed Americans are the first in line for new jobs.

Many U.S. workers have been hurt through no fault of their own as COVID-19 spread across the world. No American president should allow blue-collar or middle-class workers to remain on the sidelines and be replaced by new foreign labor.

In addition to extending the pause on new immigrant visas through the end of 2020, today’s proclamation builds on that measure with an additional pause on several job-related nonimmigrant visas, including H-1Bs.

An overwhelming majority of Americans support pausing immigration as we recover from the Coronavirus pandemic, recent polls show. With some exceptions, such as to shore up critical supply chains, we should not permit large numbers of foreign workers to enter the United States at a time when so many citizens are out of work.

“We have a moral duty to create an immigration system that protects the lives and jobs of our citizens,” President Trump says.

GET THE FACTS: Learn more about today’s immigration decision. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c1d048af4b&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
President Trump signs a proclamation suspending certain visas to protect American workers during the Coronavirus recovery | June 22, 2020

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bae45051d6&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2f3cae5319&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=8c2b1d9a37)

============================================================
The White House . 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW . Washington, DC 20500 . USA · 202-456-1111

subject: Did you see me at Trump's rally?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathaleen Wall for Congress
body:
Check out these photos from Oklahoma | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/455efaaf7c0f/trump-announces-national-emergency-for-covid-12556066?e=01c99b7fbd)
---
| |
---
| | Hi friend,

As I’m meeting you at your front door, calling you on the phone, and chatting with you on social media, I had the honor this past weekend of joining President Trump at his first campaign rally in THREE months in Tulsa, Oklahoma.

And boy what an event it was! Our President just keeps winning.

I wanted to share a few photos from the rally so you could share in our excitement for this election year.

If you want to be a part of my campaign to go to Washington D.C. as your next Congressman, email us at [Info@KathaleenWall.com](mailto:Info@KathaleenWall.com) or visit our [volunteer signup online](https://KathaleenWall.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6732b2a961d1a34b05e3194bb&id=204fbaceb1&e=01c99b7fbd) and help out today.

On to victory!

God bless you,
Kathaleen Wall
